[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: 'Disgust' as park litter experiment backfires | London Evening Standard | The Standard
[METADATA/INTRO]: 'Disgust' as park litter experiment backfires An experiment to get visitors to Hyde Park to take away their litter has left organisers "disgusted"....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Organisers stopped picking up litter and emptying bins between Speakers' Corner and the Reformers' Tree towards the centre of the park, while observing how visitors - who were not told about the experiment - reacted. 
 Keep Britain Tidy spokeswoman Helen Bingham said: "It was a real mess by this morning - it was disgusting. Lots of people have sat down on the grass and eaten or drunk or changed their baby's nappy and left it behind." 
 Some people took their rubbish away, while others wrapped it in plastic bags and left it beside overflowing bins, which became an unofficial dumping ground.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: The picture of rubbish ‘left behind by climate change protest’ exposed as fake news | The West Australian
[METADATA/INTRO]: A social media storm is brewing after critics of Friday’s climate change rallies shared a photograph of the plastic and debris left behind after the march in Sydney’s Hyde Park. The only problem was the picture wasn’t from Sydney’s Hyde...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: “Look at the mess today’s climate protesters left behind in beautiful Hyde Park. So much plastic. So much landfill,” the post, which was later removed, said. 
 But it wasn’t long before the source of the image came to light. 
 According to 7NEWS, it was in fact a picture of London’s Hyde Park after a gathering of marijuana enthusiasts in April. 
 And according to The Hemp Trading Company, the mess was cleaned up by a group of Extinction Rebellion climate protesters who had been gathering nearby. 
 More than 200,000 people across Australia have made their voices heard as part of yesterday’s international strike calling for stronger climate change action.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2019-09-27
[TITLE]: Viral Photo Falsely Targets Climate Strike Protesters - FactCheck.org
[METADATA/INTRO]: Quick Take A post on social media falsely claims to show litter left behind by Climate Strike protesters in London on Sept. 20. The photo is from a completely unrelated event in April....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The photo also received considerable attention on the Australian Youth Coal Coalition Facebook page, which has since removed a post that said: “Look at the mess today’s climate protesters left behind in beautiful Hyde Park. So much plastic. So much landfill. So sad.” 
 Here are the facts: the photo shows Hyde Park in London, not the park of the same name in Sydney, Australia. It was taken April 20, not last week. And the trash was left behind by people attending an event celebrating the unofficial marijuana holiday 420 — not a climate protest. 
 Even in April, people falsely identified activists from Extinction Rebellion, a movement fighting against climate change, as the source of the litter. Those protesters were nearby at a different event at the same time as the cannabis festival in Hyde Park. Royal Parks, the charity that manages Hyde Park, addressed those false claims in an April 23 tweet.
